Curriculum → AI Engineer learning path → Prompt engineering and RAG → Retrieval-augmented generation

Lesson 2 of 3

Retrieval-augmented generation

End-to-end RAG architecture: document ingestion, chunking, retrieval, context assembly, and grounded generation.

35-45 min AI/ML flow Prompt engineering and RAG 4 guided stops

Study map

What this lesson helps you explain

RAG is the dominant pattern for building knowledge-grounded AI applications that need up-to-date information without retraining the model.

Start here Frame the trade-off before diving into architecture. 1. RAG architecture Document loaders handle PDFs, HTML, databases, and API sources2. Advanced RAG patterns Query expansion and hypothetical document embeddings improve recall3. Evaluation and optimization Retrieval metrics: precision@k, recall@k, MRR measure retriever quality

Guided walkthrough

Move through the lesson in a clear interview order

4 steps

Start with the framing, walk the concept in order, then use the right rail for prompts, pitfalls, and visual anchors.

1. Interview framing RAG is the dominant pattern for building knowledge-grounded AI applications that need up-to-date information without retraining the model. 2. RAG architecture Document loaders handle PDFs, HTML, databases, and API sources3. Advanced RAG patterns Query expansion and hypothetical document embeddings improve recall4. Evaluation and optimization Retrieval metrics: precision@k, recall@k, MRR measure retriever quality
1

Start with this framing

Interview framing

RAG is the dominant pattern for building knowledge-grounded AI applications that need up-to-date information without retraining the model.

What to say first

  • Can design a complete RAG pipeline from document ingestion to response generation.
  • Understands chunking tradeoffs and their impact on retrieval quality.
  • Evaluates RAG systems with appropriate retrieval and generation metrics.
2

Study section

RAG architecture

Ingest documents, chunk and embed them, retrieve relevant passages at query time, and augment the LLM prompt with retrieved context.

Call these points out explicitly

  • Document loaders handle PDFs, HTML, databases, and API sources
  • Chunking strategy (sentence, paragraph, semantic boundaries) affects retrieval granularity
  • The retriever-generator pipeline separates knowledge storage from reasoning
3

Study section

Advanced RAG patterns

Query transformation, multi-step retrieval, reranking, and self-reflection improve answer quality beyond naive RAG.

Call these points out explicitly

  • Query expansion and hypothetical document embeddings improve recall
  • Multi-hop retrieval chains answer complex questions requiring multiple sources
  • Self-RAG and CRAG add verification steps that check retrieval relevance
4

Study section

Evaluation and optimization

Measuring retrieval recall, answer faithfulness, and hallucination rates guides pipeline tuning.

Call these points out explicitly

  • Retrieval metrics: precision@k, recall@k, MRR measure retriever quality
  • Generation metrics: faithfulness, relevance, and hallucination rate assess grounding
  • End-to-end evaluation combines retrieval and generation quality holistically

Checklist

Things to say clearly

  • Can design a complete RAG pipeline from document ingestion to response generation.
  • Understands chunking tradeoffs and their impact on retrieval quality.
  • Evaluates RAG systems with appropriate retrieval and generation metrics.

Common pitfalls

What to avoid

  • Using fixed chunk sizes without considering document structure.
  • Not measuring retrieval quality separately from generation quality.
  • Stuffing too much context into the prompt, exceeding useful context window usage.

Interview prompts

Practice aloud

  • Design a RAG system for a customer support knowledge base with 10,000 articles.
  • How would you handle conflicting information from multiple retrieved documents?
  • Compare naive RAG to advanced RAG with reranking. When is the complexity justified?

Related topics

Keep the thread going

Embeddings and vector search Large language models and NLPPrompt engineering Prompt engineering and RAG

Interactive topic lab

RAG pipeline design lab

Interactive diagram

Production RAG pipeline with confidence gating

Rendering diagram…

Combine multiple retrieval strategies and add confidence checks before serving generated answers.

Worked example

RAG for internal knowledge bases with mixed document types

A company has 50K internal documents (Confluence pages, PDFs, Slack threads, code docs) and wants employees to ask natural language questions.

Recommended choice

Build a multi-source ingestion pipeline with format-specific parsers, semantic chunking, and a unified vector index.

Why it fits

  • Different document types need specialized parsers (PDF layout, Slack threads, code structure).
  • Semantic chunking preserves meaning boundaries better than fixed-size splitting.
  • Metadata (author, date, team, document type) enables filtered retrieval for relevance.

If you chose differently

Treating all documents as plain text with fixed-size chunks loses structural information and mixes unrelated content in the same chunk.

Interview-ready takeaway

Quality RAG requires treating ingestion as seriously as generation. Most failures trace to poor chunking or missing metadata.

Interactive practice lab

Write, save, and move through the mock interview

Answers stay in your browser so you can come back later.

0/3 saved Step 1/3: Explain the concept
Ln 1, Col 1 1 lines 0 words 0.0 KB markdown

PRACTICE GUIDE

Explain the concept Demonstrate deep understanding by explaining the topic clearly, covering intuition, algorithms, and practical applications.

Explain the concept

Explain "Retrieval-augmented generation" as if teaching a senior engineer who is new to AI/ML. Cover the core intuition, when and why you would use it, and the key algorithms or techniques involved.

Goal

Demonstrate deep understanding by explaining the topic clearly, covering intuition, algorithms, and practical applications.

Guardrails

  • Design a RAG system for a customer support knowledge base with 10,000 articles.
  • RAG architecture
  • Advanced RAG patterns
  • Can design a complete RAG pipeline from document ingestion to response generation.

Suggested Structure

  • Core intuition
  • Key algorithms or techniques
  • When to apply
  • Common variants
